Ikonahima no Mikoto
Ameito Wake no Mikoto{{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine and Shrine Agency}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ryuzo|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=August 4th|Q2784|WD=Q2784|1=August 4}} |Shinto ritual = Mikoshi tour (once every two years)|Map = {{Location map|Japan|label=Toga Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}}{{Maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=religious-shinto}} }} {{List of coordinates}} '''Toga Shrine'' (Toga Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} is {{sfn|Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine Agency}}, and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the general guardian of {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}} and is enshrined on Mt. Toga, which stands at the southwestern tip of the island.{{Sfn|Ako Village (Heibonsha)|2002}}. === About the company name === The company name ``Toga'' is said to mean ``Toga,'' or ``the entrance to Mt. Oyama (volcano in the center of Miyakejima).''{{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. However, there is a theory that it was once pronounced as ``Fuga'' {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1981|p=77}}. In addition, Toga Shrine is located in "{{ill|lt=Records of Emperor Montoku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}", "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}", "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064 932|WD=Q11064 932|lt=Divine Name Ceremony}} This name has been compared to ``''Ametsuke no Mikoto Jinja'' (Ametsuke no Mikoto Jinja), which looks like ``Ametsuke no Mikoto Jinja'', but this name is also ``Ametsuchiwake no Mikoto Jinja''. = It is said to mean the deification of the ``peak'' that separates heaven and earth. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Kotoshiro-shu|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiro-shu no Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}''' (Kotoshironushi Mikoto) *: Mishima Daimyojin. {{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}'s {{ill|lt=Mikogami|Q352176|WD=Q352176|1=Mikogami}}. {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}}'s {{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}) enshrined deity. * '''Ikonahime no Mikoto'' (Ikonahime no Mikoto) *: Toko Daimyojin. The empress of the master of affairs. {{ill|lt=Ikonahime Shrine|Q11379183|WD=Q11379183|1=Ikonahime no Mikoto Shrine}} enshrined deity in {{ill|lt=Shimoda City|Q653402|WD=Q653402|1=Shimoda, Shizuoka}}, Shizuoka Prefecture. * '''Amet Wake no Mikoto'' *: Toga Daimyojin. The child god of Kotoshiro no Mikoto, Ikonahime no Mikoto. [[File:Mishima-taisha haiden-1.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|1=Mishima Taisha}} ({{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Mishima City|Q653478|WD=Q653478|1=Mishima, Shizuoka}})}}{{small|Mishimakami is the main deity. }}]]The two pillars of Mishima God and Ikonahime no Mikoto are said to have been enshrined at Toga Shrine in {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. The reason why Mishima no kami is now considered to be the same as Kotoshironushi no Mikoto is that in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), the deity enshrined at Mishima Taisha Shrine was changed to Kotoshironushi no Mikoto. According to {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}, the deities enshrined at the current Mishima Taisha Shrine were later changed to two: {{ill|lt=Oyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyamazumi-no-mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}} and Kotoshiro-nushi-no-mikoto. In addition, Ikonahima no Mikoto is called ``Togoku Daimyojin'' because it means ``toko = tokuwa''. The word ``Tokisaki'' is ``Tokisaki (toki: present-day Empress)'' and is considered to be synonymous with ``Later''. . === About Wake Ameito === The original Toga Shrine is said to be a shrine that enshrines Ameto Waki-no-Mikoto, as the name of the company listed in "Engishiki" (mid-Heian period) is ``Ameto (Wa) Ki-no-Mikoto'' {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. However, the details regarding the enshrined deity Ameto Wake no Mikoto are not clear, and there are various theories as follows. One theory is that in ``{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}'' (late Kamakura period), the name of the god ``''Tenchi Imamiya'''' is seen as the place where Mishima no kami placed Miyakejima, so this is believed to be ``Tenchi.'' There is a theory that it means Ameto Wake no Mikoto because it is a misprint of ``Bun'''' Empress. On the other hand, there is a theory that ``Empress Tenchi Imamiya'' refers to Ikonahima no Mikoto, and that her child was ``Tenchi Imamiya'', that is, Ameto Wake no Mikoto, so she was named ``Empress Tenchi Imamiya'' {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine) | 1981|p=83}}. At Toga Shrine, it is said that during ancient rituals, people would chant, ``Ichidaisha, '''Ametsuchi Imamiya'', Imago,'' which refers to Mishima God, Ametsu Wake no Mikoto, and Ikonahime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=82}}. However, the children of Empress Tenchi Imamiya recorded in "Miyakeki" are two princes, ``Annei (rice prince)'' and ``Mannei (sake prince)'', and this theory has been questioned since the name of ``Ametsuchi Imamiya'' is not seen {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine) | 1981|p=83}}. Although there are various theories mentioned above, in the current Toga Shrine, Ameto Wake-no-Mikoto is said to be the son of Ikonahime-no-Mikoto. === History === [[File:Miyake-jima 20010401014415.jpg|thumb|180px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}}}]] === Founding === Founding date is unknown. According to the shrine legend, Kotoshiro no Mikoto (Mishima no Kami) traveled with his father {{ill|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} from {{ill|lt=Izumo Province|Q582846|WD=Q582846|1=Izumo Province}} to {{ill|lt=Ki Province|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}, and then to {{ill|lt=Miyakejima|Q1075076|WD=Q1075076|1=Miyake-jima}}. It is said that they introduced fishing and agriculture and built the foundation of the island. It is also said that when Iga Muhime no Mikoto arrived at this island being chased by a large snake, the large snake was exterminated by the order of Kotosushi no Mikoto {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine Journal Materials|1912}}. There is a long-established tradition, including the ``{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}'', that Mishima-kami (the deity enshrined at Mishima Taisha Shrine) in Mishima City, Shizuoka Prefecture was enshrined on the Izu Islands, especially Miyakejima, and there is also a theory that our company is the birthplace of Mishima-kami.There is a theory that the origin of Miyakejima's name is ``Miyakejima'', and the origin of Miyakejima is closely related to eruptions, and it is speculated that Toga Shrine was founded as a shrine dedicated to the eruption as a deity. One theory is that Toga Shrine was originally enshrined at Toga-daira, the 8th station of Mt. Oyama.{{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. However, due to the eruption, the shrine building was destroyed by fire, so Arashima Shrine ({{Coord|34|4|14.27|N|139|28|53.28|E|re gion:JP-13_type:landmark|name=Arashima Shrine (former enshrined place)}}), and then moved to its current location{{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. The truth or falsehood of the above legend is unclear, but since curved balls from the {{ill|lt=Kofun period|Q459939|WD=Q459939|1=Kofun period}} have been excavated from the current shrine site, it is believed that rituals have been performed here since ancient times as a place of distant worship {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. === Outline === In the national history, in "{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor's Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}", 3 years ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) , "Ameto Wake no Mikoto" was conferred to {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}, lower than the junior fifth rank, and in {{ill|lt=Jinju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}}2nd year ({{ill|lt=852 years|Q30383|WD=Q30383|1=852}}), the same god rose to the upper rank of Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank) {{Sfn|Akomura (Heibonsha)|2002}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}" In {{ill|lt=Izu Province|Q860928|WD=Q860928|1=Izu Province}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District|Q961511|WD=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, it is described as ``Ame Toki no Mikoto Shrine,'' and it is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}{{Sfn|Ako Village (Heibonsha)|2002}}. There are 12 Shikinai shrines on Miyakejima, and Toga Shrine is the core of them {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}.According to ``{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}},'' which is said to be written at the end of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|WD=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, Mishima no kami created the islands of Izu and decided to place a queen on each island, and placed ``the queen of Tenchi Imamiya'' on Miyakejima. Afterwards, it is said that Mishima no Kami received three daughters from Okina of Hakone and placed them on Miyakejima. The gods are said to be Igai no Gozen (Igaya's Goto Shrine), Ninomiya Gozen (Tsubota's Ninomiya Shrine), and Hachioji's Mother Gozen (Kamitsuki's Mishaku Shrine). The history of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is not clear, but there are Japanese mirrors from the Kamakura and Muromachi periods in the inner sanctum of the main hall, as well as a hanging Buddha believed to be from the end of the Kamakura period. The subsequent history is as follows:{{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1981|p=80}} * In May of {{ill|lt=Horeki|Q1194397|WD=Q1194397|1=Hōreki}}9 ({{ill|lt=1759|Q7643|WD=Q7643|1=1759}}), the shrine building was restored. * In the first year of {{ill|lt=Yasunaga (era name)|Q1193910|WD=Q1193910|lt=Yasunaga|1=An'ei}} ({{ill|lt=1772|Q7690|WD=Q7690|1=1772}}), the shrine building was burnt down. * In February of Yasunei 5 ({{ill|lt=1776|Q7703|WD=Q7703|1=1776}}), the shrine building was rebuilt. * In June of {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}}), the shrine building was restored. * In {{ill|lt=Tenpo|Q1369045|WD=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}}6 ({{ill|lt=1835|Q7597|WD=Q7597|1=1835}}), the shrine building was restored.* In December of {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|WD=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1849|Q7650|WD=Q7650|1=1849}}), Mikado (Sangodo) was restored. * In the 3rd year of Kaei ({{ill|lt=1850|Q7653|WD=Q7653|1=1850}}), the four Setsusha companies were revived. * In April of {{ill|lt=Bunkyu|Q730266|WD=Q730266|1=Bunkyū}}2 ({{ill|lt=1862|Q7698|WD=Q7698|1=1862}}), the mikoshi was repaired. * In May of {{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761|1=1874}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}7), it was promoted to {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in the {{ill|lt=Modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|WD=Q2024|1=2007}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510|1=Heisei era}}19), restoration of shrine building [http://www.miyakejima.com/toga/ Toga Shrine] (official website). . === God level === * June 4, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}), Jugoinoge ("{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}") June 4, 850, "Japanese Emperor Montoku Annals" article. - The notation is "Ameto Wake no Mikoto." * December 15, 2nd year of {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} ({{ill|lt=852|Q30383|WD=Q30383|1=852}}), Upper Jugoi (``Nihon Montoku Emperor Jitsuroku'') - The notation is "Ameto Wake Life God." * {{ill|lt=Narihira|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}}June 26, 1st year ({{ill|lt=854|Q24981|WD=Q24981|1=854}}), Jugoinojo (``Nihon Montoku Emperor Jitsuroku'') - The notation is "Ameto Wake Life God." The content overlaps with the article published in Jinju 2.The reason for the overlap is unclear. There is a theory that the article written in the first year of Narihira is a typo of 'Shogoijo' (1912). . === Shinto priest === The priest of Toga Shrine is inherited from generation to generation by the Mibu clan {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=86}}. In ``{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}'', the name ``Mibu no Tate'' (Mibu no Tachi) appears as the first enshrinement of Mishima no Kami, and the Mibu clan is said to be a descendant of this house {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=86}}. Before the Edo period, the Mibu clan was not only a Shinto priest but also held an important position in the island's government (similar examples include the Maeda clan of {{ill|lt=Niijima|Q1191108|WD=Q1191108|1=Nii-jima}} and the Kurimoto clan of {{ill|lt=Mikurashima|Q1197235|WD=Q1197235|1=Mikura-jima}}) {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=87}}. === Precincts === * Main shrine - Sangensha {{ill|lt=Ruzuko|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper plate roof. It is enshrined on top of the stone steps about 100 steps from the worship hall. * Hall of Worship - Frontage 3 ken, Depth 2 ken 3 shaku, {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}}. On the back of the shrine, there is a shrine with a length of 1 inch per inch. * Gakudo - Frontage 3 ken, Depth 2 ken {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1981|p=87}}. Before the Meiji period, there was a kyodo hall in the precincts {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1981|p=88}}. === Setsusha === There are four shrines within the precincts: Commonly known as ``'''Shisha no Miya''''. The former shrines of the three shrines, Wakamiya, Kenmiya, and Mimemiya, are located on the side volcanoes surrounding Arashima Shrine (former headquarters of the shrine) {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. ; Wakamiya :* Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Monoimina no Mikoto|Q11570647|WD=Q11570647|lt=Monoimina no Mikoto|1=Monoiminanomikoto Shrine}} (Monoimina no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai)|1981|p=83}} : "Wakamiya". The enshrined deity is a male deity, {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}, who is a vassal of Mishima no kami as seen in "Miyakeki." The original shrine is Wakamiya Shrine {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}} ({{Coord|34|3|40.04|N|139|29|1.35|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|name=Wakamiya Shrine (Wakamiya Motomiya)}}). On the Wakamiya Shrine information board, the enshrined deity is Monoimina no Mikoto . In "Miyakeki", the three gods Wakamiya, Tsurugi, and Memi are said to be the children of {{ill|lt=Amagiya-no-Mikoto|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Amagoyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, but in the statement book they are said to be the children of Kojiro-no-shu-no-Mikoto {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1981|p=83}}. ; Sword Palace :* Enshrined deity: Unknown {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=84}}: "Tsurugi no Miya". Like Wakamiya, the enshrined deity is a male god, {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}, a vassal of Mishima. The main shrine is Sashide Shrine {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}} in Sabigahama (Sashide Jinja, {{Wiki coordinates|34|4|2.85|N|139|28|57.55|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|location|name=Sashide Shrine (Kengu Motomiya)}}). The explanation board for Sashide Shrine says that the enshrined deity is the god of swords (Tsurugi no Kami), who is the younger brother of Monokina no Mikoto. It is said that this god of swords was the first to slay a large snake in the ``Miyakeki,'' and that he used the ``snake-cutting sword'' passed down at Toga Shrine. It is also said that the place name ``Sabigahama'' came from the place where the rust was removed from swords, and ``sword'' refers to a long and narrow lava flow. ; Mimemiya :* Enshrined deity: Unknown {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=84}}: "Mimenomiya". Like Wakamiya, the enshrined deity is a vassal of Mishima, {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}, and a goddess. The main shrine is Hitoyori Shrine {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}} (Hodori Jinja, {{Wiki coordinates|34|4|25.73|N|139|28|54.71|E|region:JP-13_type:landmark|location|name=Hitoyori Shrine (Mimemiya Motomiya)}}). The explanation board for Hitoyori Shrine states that the enshrined deity is the male deity {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi no Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}. Kagu Tsuchikami is a god who creates swords, and it is said that he stretched out a divine sword and made it when he killed a large snake. Also, on Miyakejima, the fumarole of an eruption is called ``Hodo'', so Hitoyori Shrine is also said to be a shrine that enshrines the crater of Mt. Koshiki. ; Mibu Shrine :* Enshrined deity: Mibu no Mitachi {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1981|p=83}} : "Mibunomiya". The enshrined deity is the first priest of Mishima-kami, who appears in "Miyake-ki," and the founder of the Mibu family {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. === Festival === The current annual festival is August 4th, and once every two years, a mikoshi is held from the 4th to the 9th {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. This mikoshi was arranged in {{ill|lt=1883|Q7816|WD=Q7816|1=1883}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}16), and was previously held on Tori Day in November {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. The ritual involves going around the five former villages of Ako, Igaya, Izu, Kanki, and Tsubota on Miyakejima clockwise, spending one night each.{{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}} This festival is designated as an intangible folk cultural property by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government as the ``Mikoshi Tour of Toga Shrine.'' Before the war, it is said that on the 1st and 15th of every month, a tour of the island of the treasured sword used to slay giant snakes was held as ``Otachi-sama''. === Cultural assets === === Tokyo designated intangible folk cultural property === * Toga Shrine Tour Mikoshi - Designated on March 21, 2012 [http://www.soumu.metro.tokyo.jp/14miyake/miyakehp/e-shucchoujo/sub2.html Miyake Village Cultural Property] (Tokyo Metropolitan Government Bureau of Internal Affairs and Communications homepage). . === Others === The main shrine is divided into three rooms, and the following items are housed in each inner shrine {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. * Mishima Daimyojin - Hanbutsu (seated statue of Yakushi Nyorai), 3 swords. The sword is said to have been used to slay a large snake. * Togoku Myojin - 13 Japanese mirrors (4 complete mirrors). * Toga Myojin - 3 Japanese mirrors (no complete items), bronze hoko fragments. Most of the Japanese mirrors are from the Heian period, but there are also some from the Kamakura and Edo periods {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. In addition, Toga Shrine has passed down to the present day documents such as ``{{ill|lt=Miyakeki|Q17215100|WD=Q17215100|1=Miyakeki}}'', ``Miyakejima Shinmyocho'', ``Miyakejima Shrine Kaku'', ``Miyakejima Shrine Dimension Book Book'', and ``Mibu Family Annual Events'' {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. Many of them are stored in the Shichishima Bunko {{Sfn|Toga Shrine (Gods)|1987}}. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|WD=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Miyake Village|Q1154797|WD=Q1154797|1=Miyake, Tokyo}}Ako '''Access''' * Get off at the Miyake Village Bus "Toga Shrine-mae" bus stop (approx. 5 minutes walk after getting off) === Footnotes === {{Footnote Motomiya of Kotodaishu no Mikoto. - The former shrine of Ikonahime no Mikoto. * {{Cite web|Japanese book|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/tosho/6111/|title=Toga Shrine|publisher=Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine and Shrine Agency|accessdate=2020-07-21|ref=harv}} === External link === * [https://miyakejima-jinja.jimdofree.com/ Shrines on Miyakejima (Toga Shrine, Miyake Shrine, Shiitori Shrine, etc.)] - Official website * [http://tosho.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/miyakejima.html Toga Shrine] - Tokyo Metropolitan Shrine and Shrine Agency (Islands Special Edition) * [https://www.miyakejima.gr.jp/see/togashrine/ Toga Shrine] - Miyakejima Tourism Association {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Toga Shrine (Q17229311)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Toga Shrine (Q17229311)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shrines in the Islands of Tokyo Metropolis]] [[Category:三宅村の建築物]] [[Category:三宅村の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Mishima–Ōyamazumi cult]]
